comments.message: Generated file has uncommitted hash change. Run `dart run build_runner build` to ensure this matches the current `state_man.dart` source, then commit together with the source change.

Catching bare `OSError` is broad — it covers `FileNotFoundError`, `PermissionError`, etc., not just network errors. Consider narrowing to `ConnectionError` (parent of Reset/Aborted) plus `asyncio.CancelledError` if you want tighter scoping. Acceptable as-is for an SSE streaming loop where any I/O failure means 'stop streaming'.
Good fix: the previous code called `mark_running` then fell through to `mark_failed` for *all* non-pass statuses, incorrectly failing genuinely running stories. The new branching is correct.
The `import logging` inside the test function is redundant — `logging` is already available at module scope (used by caplog). Minor style nit, no functional impact.

Broken duplicate test file: Story 6 added required constructor params (dbusClient, serviceName, objectPath) to ConfigEditDialog, ConfigListPage, and ConnectionsPage stubs, but this older test file was not updated. It has 5 analysis errors and fails to compile. Either delete this file (since test/core/web_stubs_test.dart covers the same stubs correctly) or update it to pass the required arguments.

Duplicate test coverage: test/core/web_stubs_test.dart and test/pages/web_stub_test.dart test identical behavior (all 6 stub widgets render 'Not available on web'). One should be deleted. Recommend keeping test/core/web_stubs_test.dart (the working one) and removing test/pages/web_stub_test.dart.

Story 6 AC requires conditional imports for all 6 native-only pages, but config_edit, config_list, and ipc_connections are not conditionally imported in main.dart. These pages are only instantiated from dbus-dependent code paths not reachable on web, so this is functionally safe, but diverges from the plan's literal requirement.
Empty leftover file 'mytest.dart' (0 bytes) committed in the Story 6 diff. Should be deleted — appears to be a debug artifact.

_stream_json_to_log has no top-level try/except. If log_file_handle.write() or .flush() raises (disk full, closed handle), the reader thread dies silently and stops draining proc.stdout. The subprocess will then block on stdout writes, hanging until the 1800s timeout in _execute_claude. Wrap the for-loop body in try/except Exception with a logged warning, or at minimum catch IOError to keep draining the pipe.

`git add -A` in _merge_branch stages everything in the project directory — including any .env files, large binaries, or editor temp files that happen to exist. Consider using a more targeted approach (e.g., `git add -u` to only stage tracked files) or adding a .gitignore check. The comment says 'generated files left by a previous story's merge' — if the scope is that narrow, `git add -u` would be safer.

Same issue as the events handler (review b7f3): bare `except Exception` swallows programming errors (KeyError, TypeError, AttributeError) in the log-tail streaming loop. Consider catching `(ConnectionError, asyncio.CancelledError, OSError)` to only suppress expected disconnect errors.

The log-tail SSE loop sends each line via `new_data.splitlines()`. If a line is empty (blank line in log output), it produces `data: \n\n` which is a valid but empty SSE event. Not a bug, but frontend consumers should handle empty data events. Also, if a log write is mid-line (no trailing newline yet), `splitlines()` will emit a partial line — the next read will emit the remainder as a separate event, splitting one logical line across two SSE events.

discover_plans catches bare Exception and silently skips. This hides real problems (e.g., PermissionError on a valid plan file, or a plan with a subtle schema error). Consider logging a warning with the filename and exception, similar to how derive_node_statuses logs warnings for unknown statuses.

The plan_arg.is_file() check silently treats a nonexistent path as a directory. If a user typos a plan path (e.g., `plans/mqtt_wb.yaml`), this passes the nonexistent parent to run_dashboard which will raise 'No valid plan files found' — a confusing error. Consider validating the resolved plans_dir exists before passing it through.
